Z@d7ZAe
BeAd	d	
ZDd8d9
ZEd:d;
ZFeGd<k
)=a$
Calendar printing functions
Note when comparing these calendars to the ones printed by cal(1): By
default, these calendars have Monday as the first day of the week, and
Sunday as the last (the European convention). Use setfirstweekday() to
set the first day of the week (0=Monday, 6=Sunday).
repeat)

d&d d!
    Base calendar class. This class doesn't do any formatting. It simply
    provides data to subclasses.
    r
Calendar.__init__c
_firstweekdayr!
getfirstweekday
Calendar.getfirstweekdayc
Calendar.setfirstweekdayc
        Return an iterator for one week of weekday numbers starting with the
        configured first one.
        rK
iterweekdays
Calendar.iterweekdaysc
        Return an iterator for one month. The iterator will yield datetime.date
        values and will always iterate through complete weeks, so it will yield
        dates outside the specified month.
        N)
itermonthdays3r0
itermonthdates
Calendar.itermonthdatesc
        Like itermonthdates(), but will yield day numbers. For days outside
        the specified month the day number is 0.
        rK
days_before
days_afterr
itermonthdays
Calendar.itermonthdaysc
        Like itermonthdates(), but will yield (day number, weekday number)
        tuples. For days outside the specified month the day number is 0.
        rK
	enumeraterg
itermonthdays2
Calendar.itermonthdays2c
        Like itermonthdates(), but will yield (year, month, day) tuples.  Can be
        used for dates outside of datetime.date range.
        rK
endrc
Calendar.itermonthdays3c
        Like itermonthdates(), but will yield (year, month, day, day_of_week) tuples.
        Can be used for dates outside of datetime.date range.
        rK
itermonthdays4
Calendar.itermonthdays4c
        Return a matrix (list of lists) representing a month's calendar.
        Each row represents a week; week entries are datetime.date values.
        c
datesr
z/Calendar.monthdatescalendar.<locals>.<listcomp>r
listrd
monthdatescalendar
Calendar.monthdatescalendarc
        Return a matrix representing a month's calendar.
        Each row represents a week; week entries are
        (day number, weekday number) tuples. Day numbers outside this month
        are zero.
        c
daysr
z/Calendar.monthdays2calendar.<locals>.<listcomp>r
monthdays2calendar
Calendar.monthdays2calendarc
        Return a matrix representing a month's calendar.
        Each row represents a week; days outside this month are zero.
        c
z.Calendar.monthdayscalendar.<locals>.<listcomp>r
monthdayscalendar
Calendar.monthdayscalendar
        Return the data for the specified year ready for formatting. The return
        value is a list of month rows. Each month row contains up to width months.
        Each month contains between 4 and 6 weeks and each week contains 1-7
        days. Days are datetime.date objects.
        c
z.Calendar.yeardatescalendar.<locals>.<listcomp>r8
months
widthr
Januaryro
yeardatescalendar
Calendar.yeardatescalendarc
        Return the data for the specified year ready for formatting (similar to
        yeardatescalendar()). Entries in the week lists are
        (day number, weekday number) tuples. Day numbers outside this month are
        zero.
        c
z.Calendar.yeardays2calendar.<locals>.<listcomp>r8
yeardays2calendar
Calendar.yeardays2calendarc
        Return the data for the specified year ready for formatting (similar to
        yeardatescalendar()). Entries in the week lists are day numbers.
        Day numbers outside this month are zero.
        c
z-Calendar.yeardayscalendar.<locals>.<listcomp>r8

    similar to the UNIX program cal.

	oldlocale

	__enter__&
different_locale.__enter__c
argsr
__exit__*
different_locale.__exit__N)
    This class can be passed a locale name in the constructor and will return
    month and weekday names in the specified locale. If this locale includes
    an encoding all strings containing month and weekday names will be returned
    as unicode.
    r

--widthr)
z width of date column (default 2))

--linesr(
z)number of lines for each week (default 1)z

z"spacing between months (default 6)z
--monthsrv
